status_detail: v3 header-simplification fix did NOT solve the problem -- but root cause is finally diagnosed. v2's '0 response headers' symptom was NOT an engine H2 emission bug; it is browser CORS rejection. Chrome console explicitly: "Access to fetch at 'https://ai.api.gamma.app/ai/v2/generation' from origin 'https://gamma.app' has been blocked by CORS policy: The value of the 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials' header in the response is '' which must be 'true' when the request's credentials mode is 'include'." Both /ai/v2/generation and /ai/v2/render-generation now labeled '(failed) CORS error' in Network panel. Because gamma frontend uses credentials: 'include', Chrome strict-CORS strips entire response (body + headers) when ACAC is not exactly 'true' -- so v2 also showed 0 headers for the same reason, NOT because of Cache-Control / X-Accel-Buffering. v3's changes (Cache-Control: no-store, removed X-Accel-Buffering, added X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff) were therefore irrelevant.
eventstream_events_seen: no (0 events -- fetch promise rejected before any chunk readable)
errors_observed: Console: Access to fetch at 'https://ai.api.gamma.app/ai/v2/generation' from origin 'https://gamma.app' has been blocked by CORS policy: The value of the 'Access-Control-Allow-Credentials' header in the response is '' which must be 'true' when the request's credentials mode is 'include'.
Uncaught (in promise) TypeError: Failed to fetch
Network panel labels both /ai/v2/generation and /ai/v2/render-generation as '(failed) CORS error'
root_cause_hypothesis: Real missing piece is `Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true` (and likely `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: https://gamma.app` echoing the Origin instead of '*'). Because gamma's frontend uses credentials: 'include', Chrome strict-CORS strips the entire response (body + headers) when ACAC is not exactly 'true'. Engine v4 must inject ACAC=true on the PII-block intercepted response. ChatGPT likely works because it either sets ACAC=true or its frontend uses credentials: 'omit'.
recommendation_for_dev: Engine v4 for gamma must add Access-Control-Allow-Credentials: true on the intercepted PII-block response, and Access-Control-Allow-Origin should echo Origin (https://gamma.app) instead of '*'. Compare with chatgpt intercept response headers as the working baseline.
notes: Cycle 95 R-1 -- v3 header simplification verify. Result is FAIL but extremely informative: re-frames the entire problem from 'engine emits 0 headers' to 'browser strips them due to missing ACAC'. Subagent duration 6m exceeded 5min soft, within 8min hard.
